title: Aurora Galactica And Platinum Project Guide
strapline: A current, hosted map of the project: what Platinum is, what Aurora owns, how the release lanes work, what is automated, and which maintained docs are the source of truth.
currentGoal: Keep Platinum Release 1 stable, close the small Aurora trust-fix cluster, and keep the platform, applications, hosted docs, and release lanes aligned as one disciplined system.

cards.title: What Shipped
cards.body: `1.2.0 - Platinum Release 1` shipped Aurora Galactica as the first playable game on Platinum, established hosted `/dev`, kept hosted `/beta` and hosted `/production` explicit, and made the shell, picker, and shared docs part of the product rather than internal scaffolding.

cards.title: What The Platform Owns
cards.body: Platinum owns the shell, release framing, hosted lanes, pack selection path, shared services, and the first-class hosted documentation surfaces that explain the platform and release model.

cards.title: What The Application Owns
cards.body: Aurora owns Aurora gameplay: scoring, stage flow, challenge cadence, capture and rescue, dual-fighter behavior, enemy behavior, and game-specific content.

cards.title: Current Focus
cards.body: The main work is now post-release discipline: small Aurora trust fixes, a stronger platform-versus-application documentation split, and keeping localhost, hosted `/dev`, hosted `/beta`, and hosted `/production` aligned.

sections.title: Release Ladder
sections.summary: How work is expected to move through the system.
bullets: local `localhost` is the active engineering lane and the source of the current candidate.
hosted `/dev` is the shared integration preview of the current stable local candidate.
hosted `/beta` is the reviewed release-candidate lane.
hosted `/production` is the public promise and should move only from an approved hosted `/beta` candidate.
A meaningful `x.y` release should now include a complete documentation pass before hosted `/production` promotion.

sections.title: Platform Versus Applications
sections.summary: The architectural split we need to preserve.

cards.title: Platinum
cards.body: Platinum owns shared shell framing, runtime hosting, pack selection, shared services, release tooling, and the hosted documentation and release surfaces that apply to more than one game.

cards.title: Aurora Galactica
cards.body: Aurora is the first shipped application on Platinum. It owns game rules, challenge and scoring behavior, capture and rescue, and Aurora-specific content and gameplay harnesses.

cards.title: Galaxy Guardians
cards.body: Galaxy Guardians is currently a preview-only sibling application shell. Its job right now is to prove the pack-selection and identity path without pretending the gameplay is already there.

sections.title: Automation-First Gating
sections.summary: The release process should prefer automation over memory or manual estimation whenever practical.
bullets: Build and publish preflight should fail if the hosted docs set is incomplete.
Gameplay, shell, and platform boot behavior should be covered by focused harnesses whenever the behavior is stable enough to automate.
Hosted lane verification should confirm live labels and, when relevant, live input behavior.
Manual review should mostly be reserved for feel, visual quality, and presentation clarity where automation is still weaker.

sections.title: Likely Next Steps
sections.summary: The work that most naturally follows the current shipped state.
bullets: Bundle the small Aurora trust fixes into the `1.2.1` patch line.
Keep Platinum shell and docs discipline boring and reliable.
Strengthen the platform/application contract before the second application becomes fully playable.
Use the smallest useful `Galaxy Guardians` gameplay slice to prove the platform seam rather than rushing a second public launch.
